calendar.matters:
Petition For Order To Terminate Trust; And For Instructions Regarding Plan For Distribution And Cred
Off Calendar Jun-15-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brian Connors, via Zoom, for and with James Bratt, and Jessica Wienand, not present, Objectors; Chad Dorr, via Zoom, for and with Georgia Williams-Bratt and Kevin Lowry, Petitioners; Deanna Bratt, via Zoom, in propria persona, Objector; Julianna Ogle via Zoom, in propria persona, Interested Party.

The court confirms with Mr. Connors and Deanna Bratt they do not intend to file renewed objections to the amended petition and the court should treat their objections to the superseded petition on file as their renewed objections to the amended petition. The court encourages the parties appearing in propria persona to hire an attorney and for the trustees to provide the beneficiaries with an informal accounting for clarification or the court will consider ordering a formal accounting under probate code 17206. Mr. Dorr reports that there have been efforts to provide an accounting but that the poorly drafted trust has been an issue. The parties and the court discuss the possible sale of the real property with all parties agreeing that it would benefit them all if the property were sold and the sale proceeds distributed. The court offers its tentative ruling to take this matter under submission with the intent to allow for the sale of the real property. No objections are presented. The court queries whether ADR has been discussed. Mr. Dorr does not believe ADR is appropriate at this time due to the number of beneficiaries associated with this matter. The Court asked each appearing counsel or party whether they were requesting an evidentiary hearing or trial on any issue. All the parties waived an evidentiary hearing or trial and asked the Court to rule on the pleadings on file.

After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.
